_GetLibVersion.doc: Returns the audio engine's build/version number (integer, no arguments). Used repeatedly in real scripts as a feature-gate, e.g. `if Sound._GetLibVersion() >= 11 then` to choose between older and newer music/reverb APIs. Leading underscore suggests an internal accessor, but it is genuinely used by shipped code.

AutoLoad.doc: Returns a boolean used as a gate to skip the shell/menu flow and auto-load directly into the game; called with no arguments.

FinishedShell.doc: Returns whether the shell has finished; called defensively (`if Sys.FinishedShell and Sys.FinishedShell() then`), suggesting it may not exist on every build.

ForceNextAutosave.doc: Forces the next autosave to occur; called with no arguments.

GetAssetRequestMax.doc: Returns the maximum number of concurrent asset requests; used both to cache the original throttle value and as a comparison bound against pending asset operations.

GetCharacterTemplate.doc: Returns a character-template name string; used as a fallback in a chain ending in the hardcoded default `"mattias"`.

GetForceNewGame.doc: Returns whether a new game should be forced; called defensively (`if Sys.GetForceNewGame and Sys.GetForceNewGame() then`).

GetINIBriefing.doc: Returns a boolean INI-briefing setting; called with no arguments, widely used in shell/briefing flow.

GetLanguage.doc: Returns the current language as a string; can return nil/falsy on some builds — the one confirmed call site uses an `or "English"` fallback.

GetLevelName.doc: Returns the current level name as a string; called with no arguments, very widely used (including compared/lower-cased at call sites).

GetMasterScriptName.doc: Returns the master script name string; used alongside GetLevelName to drive Net.StartServer(...) and LevelBootstrap.LoadLevel(...) calls.

GetPlatform.doc: Returns a numeric platform ID (treated as a number at the call site, not a string); called with no arguments.

GetShellCode.doc: Returns the shell code string; called with no arguments.

GetSkipMission.doc: Returns the mission name to skip to, as a string; called with no arguments.

GetVersion.doc: Returns two values, `sCode, sData = Sys.GetVersion()`, describing the build version.

GuidToString.doc: Converts a GUID handle (`uGuid`) to its string form. Very widely used, often as a table key (e.g. hashing a state/node name) or for debug printing.

HaveActiveProfile.doc: Called with no arguments, presumably to check for an active save profile; its return value is discarded at the only confirmed call site, so its actual use is unconfirmed.

IsConfirmOnCircle.doc: Returns whether the confirm button follows the PlayStation-style 'circle to cancel' convention rather than 'cross to confirm' — a region/platform-specific controller setting. Very widely used, almost always called guarded (`if Sys.IsConfirmOnCircle and ...`).

IsDemoMode.doc: Returns whether the build is running in demo mode; called defensively (`if Sys.IsDemoMode and Sys.IsDemoMode() then`).

IsFinalConfig.doc: Returns whether this is a final (non-debug) build; called defensively, used to gate a debug-only skip button so it's unavailable in final builds.

IsGermanSKU.doc: Returns whether this is the German SKU; called unguarded (unlike most other Is* queries on this page) to gate region-specific content, presumably gore/violence toggles for the German release.

IsLoadingOrStreaming.doc: Returns whether the game is currently loading or streaming assets; called defensively, used to skip certain per-frame logic while loading.

LTIGetPrecacheBypass.doc: Returns a number (not a plain boolean) indicating precache-bypass state; compared against 0 (`if Sys.LTIGetPrecacheBypass() > 0 then`) to decide whether to bypass LTI precaching.

MainTime.doc: Returns the current time with no arguments, used as a fallback session-time source. Presumed to be the engine's internal pausable/scaled clock in seconds, paired with MainTimeStamp; whether it pauses or is affected by SetTimeScale is unconfirmed.

MainTimeStamp.doc: Captures a timestamp handle used the same way as RealTimeStamp (e.g. for session/task timers). Presumed tied to the engine's internal pausable/scaled 'main' clock rather than a real-world clock, though the exact difference from RealTimeStamp is unconfirmed.

NoHud.doc: Returns whether the HUD should be hidden; called unguarded to gate HUD-manager update logic.

PlayIntroMovies.doc: Returns whether intro movies should play; used to gate movie playback during bootstrap.

RealTimeStamp.doc: Captures a real-world-clock timestamp handle with no arguments, e.g. `uLockTimer = Sys.RealTimeStamp()`, later read via Sys.TimeStampGetElapsed().

RequestAutosave.doc: Requests an autosave: `Sys.RequestAutosave(bInMission, bLastMission, fMissionTime, nPercentCompleted)`. Sometimes called defensively (checked for existence first), suggesting it may be an optional/late-bound entry point not present on every build.

RequestGameState.doc: Requests a game-state transition given a free-form state-name string, e.g. "Shell", "ingame", "cinematic", "Pause", "Loading", "WaitForTether", "PDA". This is the core state-transition entry point, used dozens of times across shell/bootstrap code; state names aren't enumerated as constants anywhere, and it can return a success boolean.

RequiredAsset.doc: Registers an asset as required for the current level: `Sys.RequiredAsset(sAssetName, sAssetType, nPriority, bFlag)`. Confirmed for both "layer" and "script" asset types with a negative priority in both observed cases; the trailing boolean's meaning is not confirmed.

RumbleEnabled.doc: Returns whether controller rumble is enabled; read as part of the pause-screen options-menu system settings.

SetAssetRequestMax.doc: Sets the maximum number of concurrent asset requests: `Sys.SetAssetRequestMax(nMax)`. Used by the layer manager to temporarily raise the asset-streaming throttle and later restore the original value.

SetINIBriefing.doc: Sets the INI-briefing boolean setting: `Sys.SetINIBriefing(bValue)`. Confirmed used to toggle it via `SetINIBriefing(not GetINIBriefing())`.

SetLuaSaveVersion.doc: Sets the Lua save-data version: `Sys.SetLuaSaveVersion(nVersion)`. Always called once during bootstrap with the return value of a local GetSaveDataVersion() helper.

SetNumberOfViewports.doc: Sets the number of active viewports: `Sys.SetNumberOfViewports(n)`; confirmed called with 1.

SetSkipMission.doc: Sets the mission to skip to by name: `Sys.SetSkipMission(sMissionName)`; also confirmed called with an empty string to clear it.

SetTimeScale.doc: Sets the game's global time scale: `Sys.SetTimeScale(nScale)`. Confirmed used for a slow-motion-style effect, with the scale clamped to a minimum value before being applied.

SetTutorialsEnabled.doc: Sets whether tutorials are enabled: `Sys.SetTutorialsEnabled(bEnable)`. Setter counterpart to TutorialsEnabled; called defensively (checked for existence first) from the tutorials options-menu toggle.

StartSingleplayer.doc: Starts a singleplayer session given a level name and master script name: `Sys.StartSingleplayer(sLevelName, sMasterScript)`.

StartWithResources.doc: Returns whether to start with resources; called defensively (`if Sys.StartWithResources and Sys.StartWithResources() then`).

StringToGuid.doc: Converts a GUID string (e.g. a "0x..." hex string) to a GUID handle. Very likely the same underlying function as the bare global `StringToGuid` used pervasively elsewhere in the corpus, though the aliasing evidence is one-directional — strong for StringToGuid, unconfirmed for GuidToString.

SubtitlesEnabled.doc: Returns whether subtitles are enabled; used both guarded and unguarded, read in the pause-screen options menu.

TimeStampGetElapsed.doc: Returns elapsed seconds since a timestamp handle (from MainTimeStamp/RealTimeStamp, re-markable via TimeStampMark) was last marked. Forms a poll-based elapsed-time primitive, an alternative to the callback-based Event.TimerRelative for code that needs to check elapsed time on demand.

TimeStampMark.doc: Resets/re-marks an existing timestamp handle in place. Call sites consistently re-mark a stamp previously produced by MainTimeStamp/RealTimeStamp rather than creating a new one.

TutorialsEnabled.doc: Returns whether the tutorial-hint HUD message system is enabled. This is the gate checked in MrxTutorialManager before showing or updating a tutorial popup message, and it's read defensively in the pause-screen options-menu toggle.

YAxisInverted.doc: Returns whether the Y-axis is inverted; read alongside RumbleEnabled/SubtitlesEnabled in the same pause-screen options read.

CancelHijack.doc: Takes a plain character guid; cancels an active hijack. Observed being called on player logout/cleanup.

ClearControls.doc: Takes a plain vehicle guid; clears the vehicle's controls, called alongside turret-disable logic during hijack setup.

CloseDoor.doc: Takes a vehicle guid and a door/part name string (e.g. "pivot"); mirrors OpenDoor to close it.

EnableTurret.doc: Takes a vehicle guid, a turret/part name ("head", "main_turret"), a boolean, and sometimes an axis string ("pitch", "all") plus another boolean. Confirmed to be control/orientation-only, not weapon selection: every real call site just disables or re-enables the turret's own control during a hijack cinematic and never touches what the turret fires.

Enter.doc: Takes a vehicle guid, character guid, a seat-type string ("d", "p"), and one or two boolean flags (immediate, plus an optional extra flag). Puts a character into a vehicle by seat type rather than a specific seat guid.

EnterBySeatGuid.doc: Takes a vehicle guid, character guid, seat guid, and one or two boolean flags (immediate, plus an extra flag); returns a success boolean. Puts a character into a specific seat identified by guid.

Exit.doc: Takes a vehicle guid and a character guid, frequently with a trailing boolean flag; removes the character from the vehicle.

GetDriver.doc: Returns the driver's character guid for a vehicle uGuid, or nil if unmanned. Very widely used in real game scripts, often guarded with a nil check.

GetFromRider.doc: Takes a character uGuid; returns the vehicle guid the character is currently riding in, or nil if the character is on foot.

GetRiders.doc: Takes a vehicle uGuid and an optional seat-type filter string (e.g. "p", "g", "driver"); returns a table of rider guids.

GetSeatByType.doc: Takes a vehicle uGuid and a seat-type string ("p", "d"); at least one real call site passes a third boolean argument as well.

GetSeatFromRider.doc: Takes a character uGuid; returns the guid of the seat that character occupies.

GetSeatParams.doc: Takes a seat guid; returns a params table that includes at least an IsGunner field per observed usage.

GetSeatToSeat.doc: Takes a seat guid and a boolean; appears to return possible seat-transfer targets from that seat.

HijackAbort.doc: Takes a plain character (hijacker) guid. Part of the hijack state machine; aborts an in-progress hijack.

HijackAbortDone.doc: Takes a plain character (hijacker) guid. Part of the hijack state machine; signals that a hijack-abort has finished.

HijackComplete.doc: Takes a plain character (hijacker) guid. Part of the hijack state machine; signals a successful hijack completion.

HijackStart.doc: Takes a hijacker guid, hijackee guid, vehicle guid, and (in the one observed call site) a fourth argument that was a table/object reference (self). Begins the hijack state machine.

IsFlying.doc: Takes a plain vehicle guid; returns a boolean, checked before flight-specific hijack logic runs.

IsHijackRemote.doc: Takes a character guid; checks whether a hijack is remote. Its one real call site guards the call itself with `Vehicle.IsHijackRemote and ...`, implying the function's presence was historically uncertain in some contexts.

IsSeatALadder.doc: Takes a plain seat guid; returns a boolean for whether that seat is a ladder-type seat.

IsSeatBlocked.doc: Takes a plain seat guid; returns a boolean for whether the seat is currently blocked/unusable.

OpenDoor.doc: Takes a vehicle guid and a door/part name string (e.g. "pivot", "DriverHatch"). Using it to open a tank hatch ("DriverHatch") has been proposed as a live test but is not yet confirmed working.

SetCanPlayerUse.doc: Takes a vehicle guid, a seat-type string ("d", "a"), and a boolean; controls whether a player can use that seat type.

SetHijackState.doc: Takes a character guid and a numeric state value; sets the hijacker's state within the hijack state machine (exact state numbering not documented from call sites).

SetHijackSuccess.doc: Takes a character guid and a boolean (observed as false in the corpus); records hijack success/failure for that character.

SetParts.doc: Takes a vehicle/object guid, a part name string (e.g. "LightFront", "CtrlRotation"), and a boolean; returns a boolean. Also seen used on non-vehicle gate/alarm objects, suggesting broader applicability than just player vehicles.

SetTurretPitch.doc: Takes a vehicle guid, turret name, and a numeric pitch value (observed as 0, resetting pitch to level right after disabling the turret during hijack). Orientation only, same caveat as EnableTurret — does not affect firing.

StopTankHijackMotion.doc: Takes a plain vehicle guid; stops the tank hijack motion/animation sequence.

TransferToSeat.doc: Takes a vehicle guid, a seat guid, and a boolean flag; transfers a rider between seats.

Usable.doc: Takes a vehicle guid and a boolean; toggles whether the vehicle can be entered/used at all.

GetMaxReserveAmmo.doc: Returns the maximum reserve ammo capacity for a carried weapon guid (uWeapon). Almost always paired with SetReserveAmmo in the idiom Weapon.SetReserveAmmo(uWeapon, Weapon.GetMaxReserveAmmo(uWeapon)) to refill a weapon to full.

GetReserveAmmo.doc: Returns the current reserve ammo count for a carried/equipped weapon guid (uWeapon). Always called with a player's hand-held weapon, never a vehicle weapon.

SetReserveAmmo.doc: Sets the reserve ammo of a carried weapon guid (uWeapon) to nAmount. Commonly used to refill a weapon to max via Weapon.SetReserveAmmo(uWeapon, Weapon.GetMaxReserveAmmo(uWeapon)), but is also seen setting a flat value like 1; it can also be registered directly as an Event.Create callback (e.g. for ObjectHibernation), so it may be invoked by the engine's event dispatcher rather than called directly.

SetWidgetColor.example: _GuiInternal.SetWidgetColor(self.BasicData.uId, r, g, b, a, not bSuppressPropogation)
